svg.style: background: transparent; background-color: transparent; color-scheme: light dark;

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(226, 232, 240), rgb(34, 39, 46));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1486px; height: 1px; padding-top: 36px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #0f172a;
display: inline-block; font-size: 18px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#0f172a, #d3daea); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Figure 1. Architectural routes from long context to state-centric serving
Observed system pressure, current open-model responses, and the hypothesized Linear-first end-state

display: inline-block; font-size: 16px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#0F172A, #d3daea); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
System bottleneck
KV cache grows with context length
prefill/decode bandwidth couples
serving
agent workloads turn window size
into time-horizon cost

div.div:
Market signal
products are advertising longer service
duration
not just larger context windows
planning + tool use + continuity matter
more

div.div:
Research question
when service length T → ∞,
should memory remain token-addressable,
or become state-preserving and externally
recallable?

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(203, 213, 225), rgb(47, 56, 66));

div.div:
Full attention
strong exact token recall
best-understood
infrastructure
but memory and bandwidth
scale with history

div.div:
Hybrid
Qwen3.6 / Kimi Linear / OLMo
Hybrid
replace most layers with
recurrent or linear state
retain attention as a
precision backstop

div.div:
Sparse / MLA / SWA
DeepSeek-V2/V3.x / GLM /
sliding-window stacks
compress or localize
attention coverage
preserve token-level
addressing with better
efficiency

div.div:
Linear-first agent
stack
bounded live state
retrieval-triggered external
recall
persistent memory + tools +
compaction

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(203, 213, 225), rgb(47, 56, 66));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 762px; height: 1px; padding-top: 846px; margin-left: 371px;

div.div:
Thesis
The endgame of long context is not a bigger window; it is a longer, state-centric service
horizon.
